notes: #567 PARADIGM TESTED (do not blindly rebuild goal inference). Ran a falsifiable head-to-head on live data 2026-06-29: Arm A 'no-goals' (stable objective + live evidence -> one move, lib/experimental/state-move.ts) vs Arm B goal-engine (stored tkg_goals -> scorer). RESULT: no-goals wins. Arm B = last 5 pipeline_runs all safe_silence (145 candidates, zero winner); its tkg_goals model is 82d-frozen job-hunting goals + 50d n-gram garbage ('recurring theme has in'); its #1 risk-ranked candidate is personal homework ($346.61 Fred Meyer, risk 88) while the one objective-relevant item (Foldera/Supabase infra) sits buried at risk 52. Arm A re-ranks the SAME pool against the stated objective and surfaces the truth. CEILING (proven on live rows): 14d of inbound signal = 100% newsletters/gig-spam/bills, ZERO professional/sales/human mail -> even the winning arm can only play defense; the objective is advanced by offline/repo work the system can't see. IMPLICATION: stored goal-inference (the #567 keystone) is the wrong primitive; deprecating it is a separate later seam (tkg_goals is load-bearing across the scorer, NOT yanked from a chat); boot-hook PRODUCT TRUTH rewrite deferred until the refactor lands as its receipt. Reproduce: npx tsx scripts/experiment-state-move.ts (needs prod Supabase + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + ALLOW_PAID_LLM). Prior session shipped #577-#581. Live: Outlook + 201 Drive signals flowing; paid LLM proven working.
